overview:
**Very High fire danger holds today across the Montrose High Elevation area, driven by low humidity (23%) and elevated energy release (91st percentile).**

- Danger stays Very High through Tuesday, then eases to High Wednesday–Saturday as ERC and burning index percentiles gradually decline.
- Watchout status: 2 of 7 thresholds met (ERC and min RH), with light NE winds near 5 mph, 77°F, and no rain in the last 24 hours.
- No Red Flag Warning is posted for this area; NWS Grand Junction has only issued Hydrologic Outlooks (July 17–18).
- Montrose County remains under Stage 2 Fire Restrictions, in effect since July 1, 2026. BLM's Uncompahgre Field Office (Montrose, Delta, Mesa, Gunnison, Ouray, San Juan counties) is also under Stage 2 restrictions.
- Nearby: the Gold Mountain Fire near Ouray has burned about 37,734 acres and is 13% contained, with evacuations still active in Ouray and Gunnison counties.
- The Ferris Fire near Cahone has burned 64,881 acres and is 71% contained after merging with the Doe Canyon Fire, with evacuation status downgraded from Level 3 to Level 1 on July 17.
lead: Very High fire danger holds today across the Montrose High Elevation area, driven by low humidity (23%) and elevated energy release (91st percentile).
